assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)

All 4 files are referenced.

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: Plan Step 5 shows (plan.md lines 121-127): 'git checkout main / git pull / git checkout -b TC-9201' and 'Target branch is main (extracted from Target Branch section in Step 1)'. The task summary also states 'Branch Name: TC-9201' (plan.md line 9) and 'Target Branch: main' (plan.md line 8). Step 10 also references '--base main' for PR creation (plan.md line 218).

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md explicitly identifies both patterns: (1) Error handling — 'All handlers return Result<T, AppError> where AppError is defined in common/src/error.rs' (line 20), 'All error wrapping uses .context() for adding context to errors (anyhow-style)' (line 25); (2) Module structure — 'Each domain module (sbom, advisory, package) follows a consistent model/ + service/ + endpoints/ directory structure' (line 6). Additional patterns are also identified including naming conventions, framework choices, and test conventions.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: Plan Step 1 explicitly states (plan.md line 28): 'Target Branch: main (extracted; will be used as --base for PR creation)'. The task summary also states 'Target Branch: main' (plan.md line 8). Step 5 reiterates (plan.md line 127): 'Target branch is main (extracted from Target Branch section in Step 1)'. Step 10 uses it for PR creation (plan.md line 218): 'gh pr create --base main'.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan Step 1.5 (plan.md lines 49-54) covers this in detail: '1. Fetch all comments on TC-9201 via jira.get_issue_comments(TC-9201). 2. Search for comments starting with [sdlc-workflow] Description digest:. 3. If no digest comment found: log warning and proceed normally -- "No description digest found -- skipping integrity check. This task may have been created before digest tracking was introduced." (backward compatibility)'. The step also covers the case when a digest IS found (step 4: extract, compute, compare, stop on mismatch).
